verses.text: In the second year of Darius the word comes; the people say, 'The time is not come... that the LORD's house should be built.' Procrastination. Men find reasons to delay the work of God.
In the second year of Darius the word comes; the people say, 'The time is not come... that the LORD's house should be built.' Procrastination. Men find reasons to delay the work of God.

'Consider your ways': ye dwell in panelled houses while His lies waste; ye 'earn wages to put... into a bag with holes.' Misplaced priorities. Neglecting God's work leaves life leaking and unsatisfied.

'Consider your ways': ye dwell in panelled houses while His lies waste; ye 'earn wages to put... into a bag with holes.' Misplaced priorities. Neglecting God's work leaves life leaking and unsatisfied.

'Consider your ways': ye dwell in panelled houses while His lies waste; ye 'earn wages to put... into a bag with holes.' Misplaced priorities. Neglecting God's work leaves life leaking and unsatisfied.

'Consider your ways': ye dwell in panelled houses while His lies waste; ye 'earn wages to put... into a bag with holes.' Misplaced priorities. Neglecting God's work leaves life leaking and unsatisfied.
Again, 'Consider your ways'; go up, bring wood, build the house, 'and I will take pleasure in it, and... be glorified.' A clear call. God's glory is served when His people put His work first.
Again, 'Consider your ways'; go up, bring wood, build the house, 'and I will take pleasure in it, and... be glorified.' A clear call. God's glory is served when His people put His work first.
They looked for much and got little, for His house lay waste while they ran to their own; so He called for a drought. Withheld blessing. God's blessing follows obedience, not self-seeking.
They looked for much and got little, for His house lay waste while they ran to their own; so He called for a drought. Withheld blessing. God's blessing follows obedience, not self-seeking.
They looked for much and got little, for His house lay waste while they ran to their own; so He called for a drought. Withheld blessing. God's blessing follows obedience, not self-seeking.
Zerubbabel, Joshua, and the people obey and fear the LORD; and Haggai brings the word, 'I am with you, saith the LORD.' Sweet assurance. To those who obey, God promises His presence.
Zerubbabel, Joshua, and the people obey and fear the LORD; and Haggai brings the word, 'I am with you, saith the LORD.' Sweet assurance. To those who obey, God promises His presence.
The LORD 'stirred up the spirit' of the leaders and people, and they came and worked on His house. God moves the will. He gives both the desire and the strength to do His work.
